question:
For the eight counties listed below, what was the median number of students in $2005?$

\begin{tabular}[t]{|l|c|c|c|c|c|}
\multicolumn{6}{c}{\textbf{Number of Students per County}}\\\hline
\textbf{COUNTY}&\textbf{$2001$}&\textbf{$2002$}&\textbf{$2003$}&\textbf{$2004$}&\textbf{$2005$}\\\hline
Aiken&124&141&130&143&136\\\hline
Bamberg&17&15&15&14&11\\\hline
Barnwell&25&22&26&28&29\\\hline
Berkeley&583&557&554&553&524\\\hline
Calhoun&15&12&10&18&11\\\hline
Cherokee&19&13&18&13&19\\\hline
Chesterfield&46&18&13&22&29\\\hline
Colleton&64&49&52&46&41\\\hline
\end{tabular}

To find the median number of students in 2005 for the eight counties listed, we first need to extract the number of students for each county in that year:

- Aiken: 136
- Bamberg: 11
- Barnwell: 29
- Berkeley: 524
- Calhoun: 11
- Cherokee: 19
- Chesterfield: 29
- Colleton: 41

Next, we will list these numbers in descending order:

1. Berkeley: 524
2. Aiken: 136
3. Colleton: 41
4. Chesterfield: 29
5. Barnwell: 29
6. Cherokee: 19
7. Calhoun: 11
8. Bamberg: 11

Now, we have the ordered list of student numbers for 2005:

- 524, 136, 41, 29, 29, 19, 11, 11

Since there are 8 values (an even number), the median will be the average of the 3rd and 4th values in this ordered list.

The 3rd value is 41 and the 4th value is 29.

Calculating the median:

\[
\text{Median} = \frac{41 + 29}{2} = \frac{70}{2} = 35
\]

Thus, the median number of students in 2005 is \( \boxed{35} \).
